mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than returns following contractions (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market tightening, reducing corporate hiring uncertainty and lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE decline trigger fires over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below the trailing 12-month percentage change in the consumer price index, negative real policy rates drive yield-seeking capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect depends on the joint level of both series.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction term adds less than 1% incremental R-squared in a two-factor regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal expanding construction activity and cyclical demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, reflecting slack labor markets that allow non-inflationary growth.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not precede positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening household consumption and compresses near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are positive more than 50% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% signals an above-trend demand environment that supports earnings expansion and reduces recession risk, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 45% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand persons signals robust labor income growth and supports aggregate consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200 thousand in a month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) after month-over-month PAYEMS increases exceed 200 thousand over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points over a 3-month window signals a shift toward restrictive monetary policy, raising discount rates applied to equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS increase trigger fires over any 2-year rolling window.
